meditation.breathingTip1: 👉 Breathe in slow. Hold it. Breathe out slow.
meditation.breathingTip2: 💡 This helps your body calm down. It stops cravings.

meditations.title: Breath
meditations.guide: Sit quiet. Close eyes. Breathe normal. Count 1 to 10 as you breathe out. Start again. If your mind goes away, that is OK. Come back to counting. Do this 5 minutes. This helps stop cravings.

meditations.title: Body Scan
meditations.description: Feel your body. Let go of hurt.
meditations.guide: Lie down or sit. Close eyes. Start at your head. Feel any hurt. Breathe and let it go. Go down: face, neck, arms, chest, belly, legs, feet. Do this slow. This helps your body relax.

meditations.title: Stop Craving
meditations.guide: Sit quiet. Close eyes. Feel your craving. Don't fight it. Just watch it. Where is it? What does it feel? Imagine it like a wave. It comes up, then goes down. Cravings go away in 15-20 minutes. You can do this.

meditations.title: Love Yourself
meditations.guide: Sit quiet. Close eyes. Put hand on heart. Say: 'I am OK. I am healthy. I am happy. I will get better.' Say it slow. Feel it. Then say: 'Others will get better too.' This helps you love yourself.

tips: Start with 5 minutes. Do it every day.
Do it at the same time each day.
Cravings go away in 15-20 minutes. Sit through it.
Use meditation when you crave most.
Your mind will go away. That is OK. Come back.
See how good you get. Cravings get easier.

postureTipsList.label: Spine
postureTipsList.text: Keep it straight but not hard

header.title: Get Ready to Stop
header.subtitle: Plan now to get better. Ready people do better.

motivation.title: Why Plan?
motivation.mainMessage: Getting better is something you make happen. You have to plan.
motivation.description: Time you spend planning helps you succeed. People who plan do better.
benefits: Plans help you make good choices when it is hard
Plans make you ready and sure before you start
Plans show you hard things before they happen
Plans give you help from day one
Plans show you that you are serious about change
motivation.insight: 💡 Every minute you plan now saves you hard times later.

assess.title: 1. Know Yourself
assess.intro: Before you stop, know where you are:

questions.frequency: How much do I use? (daily, every week, many times a day)
questions.money: How much money do I spend each day or week?
questions.time: What time do I use most?
questions.triggers: What makes me want to use? (stress, bored, with people, sad)
questions.impact: How has this hurt me? (health, people, work, money)

assess.tip: Tip: Track your use for a few days. Real numbers help more than guesses.

quitDate.title: 2. Pick a Date to Stop
quitDate.mainAdvice: Pick a day in 1-2 weeks. Give yourself time to get ready. Not too long.
goodTiming: ✓ Good: After a hard time passes
✓ Good: When people can help you
badTiming: ✗ Bad: When you are stressed or sad
✗ Bad: When you are alone and can easily use
quitDate.finalAdvice: Write the date on your calendar. Tell people about it. Put it where you see it. Make it real.

triggers.title: 3. Know What Makes You Use
triggers.intro: Know what makes you want to use. Then you can plan what to do.

emotional.title: Feelings
emotional.description: Stress, bored, lonely, angry, scared, sad... what feeling makes you use?

social.title: People
social.description: Do you use with some people? In some places?

environmental.title: Places and Times
environmental.description: Times of day or places? Your room? Late night?

habitual.title: Habits
habitual.description: Things you always do? After coffee? After work?

triggers.action: For each trigger: Write what you will do instead.

support.title: 4. Get Help
support.intro: You cannot do this alone. Get help now, before you quit.

people.title: 👥 Tell People
items: Tell one person you trust. Call them anytime.
Tell someone who will not judge you
Tell someone who will be happy for you

resources.title: 📱 Get Help
items: This app - use all of it

professional.title: 🏥 Doctor Help
items: Help for brain or heart problems
Don't try alone if it is dangerous

support.action: Write 3-5 people you can call. Save their numbers now.

coping.title: 5. Plan What to Do
coping.intro: You will crave. You need tools ready.

distraction.title: 🎮 Do Something
distraction.description: Craving Game, movie, call a friend, shower, move.

physical.title: 💪 Move
physical.description: Walk, run, yoga, dance. Move your body.

journaling.title: 📔 Write
journaling.description: Write in diary. Write about cravings and feelings.

emotions.title: 🎭 Feel
emotions.description: Track moods. Name your feelings. Feel them.

connection.title: 🤝 Talk
connection.description: Call someone. Text. Go to a meeting.

coping.action: Make a list: 3-5 things you will do when you crave. Put it where you see it.

remove.title: 6. Remove Things
remove.intro: Make it hard to use. You can't use what you don't have.

clean.title: 🚭 Clean
clean.description: Get rid of all things you use with. Clean your room, car, bag.

delete.title: 🗑️ Delete
delete.description: Delete apps, block sites, delete people's numbers.

money.title: 💳 Money
money.description: Give your money to someone you trust. Remove cards.

avoid.title: 📍 Avoid Places
avoid.description: Plan different ways to go. Avoid places you use.

distance.title: 👥 Distance
distance.description: Tell friends you are quitting. Real friends will help.

fortyEightHours.title: 7. Plan First 48 Hours
fortyEightHours.intro: The first 48 hours are the hardest. Plan every hour.

wakeUp.title: First Thing
wakeUp.description: Tell someone you started. Do something healthy. Exercise, eat good, meditate.

morning.title: Morning
morning.description: Plan something. Exercise, meet a friend, hobby, meditate.

afternoon.title: Afternoon
afternoon.description: Call your helper. Use your tools. This time will pass.

evening.title: Evening
evening.description: Be with people. Stay busy. Write in diary.

night.title: Night
night.description: Bath, tea, meditate, book. You can do this.

fortyEightHours.insight: 💡 If you make 48 hours, you will probably get better for long time. You can do this.

mental.title: 8. Get Your Mind Ready
mental.intro: Your mind decides if you win. Think these things now:
beliefs: ✓ You can do this. Many people did. You can too.
✓ This is hard but it gets easier
✓ Cravings do not last long. Maybe 20 minutes
✓ You are not bad. You are brave for trying
✓ Every day you don't use makes you stronger
